Inter, Spalletti: "There was a sense of awareness from the whole team. Icardi? I hope Sampaoli never takes him..."

Spalletti finds "his" Inter: 5 goals for Samp and probably the best performance of the season. "This is the team attitude, the compactness, the strength, the bump, the muscle, the ability to manage the moments - said the Nerazzurri coach at Sky Sport 24 - understand when to attack or wait. the Samp as a collective, we did it. The aspect that reassures me the most? That the team works.It is important who makes goals, but especially those who work as did today Gagliardini who was on all the balls and gave that quality that creates moments to find outlets".

Unfortunately, the break does not come at the best time but Spalletti had this to say: "It does not change anything when it arrives, we started doing the right things again. There was an awareness of what we had to do, we will return to doing these things even after the break."

Spalletti then analyzes the long negative period: "It is often the coach's fault, when you win you let some moments pass instead of breaking the boxes because you pass like the one that annoys when things are going well I let something go by without realizing it so I'm responsible".

Finally, a curtain on the non-convocation of Icardi with Argentina: "Bravo Sampaoli, I hope you always leave Icardi available to me. If you do not include him for the World Cup, he will be motivated to prove to Sampaoli that he has made a mistake and hopefully play better".

ICARDI: "TO SCORE MY 100TH GOAL HERE WAS DESTINY BUT ONLY THE WIN MATTERS"

GENOA – Four goals took Mauro Icardi up to 100 goals in Serie A overall and then 100 in an Inter shirt from 172 appearances. But his goals along with Perisic’s strike were important above all for delivering three important points for the Nerazzurri. “We needed a match like that because we had to win today after the 0-0 with Napoli,” he said after Sampdoria 0-5 Inter. “We had the right approach and that showed with the result. The Coach knows how to manage certain moments, he provoked us a bit and we needed to show that we had quality today. Today it was on show but we can obviously improve further and we must do so.”

The captain then discussed the historic milestone that he reached in the match. “I’ve scored 100 goals in Serie A and 100 with Inter, I’m happy but I didn’t expect them to come today. I scored my first goal in the top flight here, it was destiny. But, the win was the most important thing. I also went through a difficult time with my injury, I had something bothering me and I couldn’t play without having it on my mind. Now, it’s just an unpleasant memory, I’m back available and I want to help the team."

MAURO ICARDI: THE ANATOMY OF A GOALSCORER
The Nerazzurri captain has reached 103 goals in Serie A and he's the sixth youngest player to hit 100 in Serie A


In a sport such as football, numbers might not tell the full story but they can say a lot. The figurers from Mauro Emanuel Icardi certainly express how important a player he is. With 103 goals in 180 Serie A appearances at the age of just 25 years and 27 days, he is the sixth youngest player of all-time to hit 100 in the league. Only the legends, Meazza, Piola, Boniperti, Borel and Altafini got their first.

After coming through at the Barcelona cantera, he was brought to Italy by Sampdoria and scored his first goal as a professional in Serie B. To put it simply, his impact on the top flight in the 2012-13 season was excellent. He established himself as one of the league’s most interesting prospects, scoring ten goals from 31 matches with an average of one goal every 212 minutes.

In the summer of 2013, Icardi was signed by Inter. His first goal in a Nerazzurri shirt is hard to forget given that it was Juventus in a 1-1 draw on 14th September. It was just the start for his impressive goalscoring record that continues to this day. In his first season, he scored nine and he would destroy his own record the next season with 22 goals in the 2014-15 season when he finished as top scorer. In the 2015-16 season, Icardi became Inter captain at the age of just 22 and he finished with 16 goals to his name, becoming the youngest foreign player to hit 50 goals in Serie A. The Argentina continued to score consistently. He netted 26 times last season. The landmark of 100 goals in Serie A kept approaching and it was reached in spectacular fashion thanks to his four goals against Sampdoria in Genoa. Maurito has 22 goals to his name this seasons and 103 goals overall in the Italian top flight.

Out of his 103 league goals, 65 have been with the right foot and 16 with his head. Overall, 98 of them have been scored inside the penalty area. Mauro makes more of an impact in the second half. 62 of his goals have come after the break, compared to 41 in the first half. Finally, a look at his assist figures. The players who have benefited from his assists the most are current teammates Ivan Perisic and Antonio Candreva who have been set up 13 and 9 times respectively.

100 (or rather 103) goals for Mauro…And this is just the start!

Luciano Spalletti's team returned to winning on the road with a 5-0 win at Sampdoria. 
In collaboration with Opta, here are the stats from the match. 
• Mauro Icardi has now become the sixth-youngest player to score 100 goals in Serie A.
• Icardi has also reached 100 goals for Inter in all competitions, in 172 appearances.
• Icardi bagged his 6th hat-trick in Serie A - a league-high since his debut in 2012/13.
• The last Inter player who managed to score four goals in a Serie A game was Diego Milito in 2012 vs Palermo.

• The Nerazzurri have kept three consecutive clean sheets in Serie A for the first time since December 2016.
• Inter’s last 5-0 win against Sampdoria in Serie A came in 1970.
• Inter have now hit the woodwork 17 times – at least three more than any other side in this Serie A campaign.

Lautaro Martinez’s move to Inter is just about done according to Racing Club president Victor Blanco who revealed the move is “90% done” in an interview with Argentine news outlet La Oral Deportiva.

“It’s hard for Lautaro Martinez to go to Borussia Dortmund because there is already a pre-contract with Inter, it is 90% done,” Blanco said.

Inter, Icardi's agent Wanda Nara: " sometimes make the joke that every day that passes Icardi is worth more and more​. Lautaro Martinez in July? Mauro is ready to welcome him at Appiano as he always has

Four goals scored and a new record. Mauro Icardi enters the club of the players that have scored 100 goals in Serie A. Credit goes to his sense of goals but also to his family which always stands by his side. Wanda Nara above all, wife and agent of the Argentinean striker, who gave an interview to La Gazzetta dello Sport about his Mauro: "He's quality and quantity, both on and off the field. On Sunday morning I asked him if he brought with him his 100 goals shoes. He answered "I hope I'll finally be able to wear them...". And then, during the second time.. It's been a perfect afternoon".

"100 goals in the stadium where it all began? Mauro will forever be bound to Sampdoria. He started to scored as a youngster there, on Sunday he scored 4 goals with the captain armband. What kind of father is he? Really present, he always want to plan something with the kids. When he gets back home it's an unique party, the problem is when he has to go back with the team".

"Do the fans' whistles stress him? I'd say no, they don't get to him as he remains cold on the pitch. They boo him trying to get him pissed. He really wanted to score the 100th goal because history doesn't get cancelled. He also wanted to do it for the fans. He's the first of the Inter tifosi, he knows well what that means. Mauro's future? I can't predict it, but I can say that he likes the Zhang family's plan. They want to make Inter grow and get them back between the top clubs".

"Mauro's relationship with Spalletti? Healthy and sincere as it should be between a captain and his coach. Is he one of the best strikers in Europe? The numbers say it, without speaking of technical/tactical matters. Contract renewal? We're all relaxed about it. We're confident about the extension and when I meet the sporting director at the stadium we make jokes about it. I sometimes make the joke that every day that passes Icardi is worth more and more. Lautaro Martinez in July? Mauro is ready to welcome him at Appiano as he always has".

Voted his country’s second-best player of the year in 2017, Inter defender Milan Skriniar has stated that his intention is to become a club icon, like the man who beat him to Slovakia’s number one spot, Marek Hamsik of Napoli.

The 23-year-old has had an outstanding season with the Nerazzurri since his arrival from Sampdoria last summer and speaking this week, Skriniar confirmed that; “I’m very happy at Inter, I’m very content to be here. It would be beautiful to have a career like Hamsik at Napoli,” he told journalists.

Skriniar’s performances have not gone unnoticed around Europe with Barcelona and Manchester City both reported to be interested in his services.

A lot could depend on what happens in May and whether Inter make it into next season’s Champions League. At the moment, the Nerazzurri looks to be in a three-way fight for the final slot along with city rivals Milan and Lazio.

Cuello: ‘Inter a dream’

Argentinian wonderkid Tomás Cuello admits interest from Inter - “it would be a dream to wear that shirt”.

The 18-year-old is currently playing for Atlético Tucumán, and has been called-up to the Argentinian Under-20 team in recent months.

“I like to play in a free role on the left,” Cuello told FCInterNews.

“I love going one against one, coming off the wing and getting goals. My main strength is being able to beat a man and score.

“My idol? It’s always been Leo Messi.

“Inter? It’s true, there’s a lot of talk about the Nerazzurri. The truth is that my agent is working on it, the interest of a team like Inter makes me very happy.

“It would be a dream to wear that shirt.

“I’m ready, I’d like to arrive in June, I feel ready to work hard every day to improve, and sign for Inter if God wills it.

“I guess I’d initially play in the youth teams, and from there I’d work to move up.

“My dream is to play on the Old Continent, I’d like to wear the Inter shirt and maybe one day also go to the Premier League.”

The Beneamata have Argentine connections, as Vice-President Javier Zanetti and captain Mauro Icardi are both from Cuello’s homeland.

“Javier was always a great player, I admired him on TV with Inter and the national team.

“Mauro seems incredible to me. In 2017, when I was training with the national team, I got to see his crazy abilities up close.

“It would be a dream to sign for Inter and play alongside him one day.”

autaro Martinez’s Agent: “Inter Deal Is Completely Done”

Lautaro Martinez’s agent Beto Yaque has confirmed that the deal with Inter is now completely done in an interview with Radioenlinea.com today.

“With Inter it is now completely done. We have met with them for several months but the interest surprised us. The choice of Inter is not dictated by money as Lautaro will go to the Nerazzurri for a sporting interest and they view him as an important player. The release clause will be €111 million and they will build the team around him.”

Yaque then discussed the kind of person Martinez is.

“I have known him since he was 15, he has the same personality as when he was a kid, he is an extraordinary guy and an incredible professional. Medel: "I've given my soul to Inter. I'm sorry we haven't won anything. The problem? A lof of confusion"

Gary Medel, grinta, heart and longs. The former Inter midfielder has given an interview to La Gazzetta dello Sport. From Inter to Besiktas, the team where he decided to move last summer and with which he's currently third in the Super Lig, the Turkish league, four points away from Galatasaray who's on top. Many memories for the player who remains bound to his former team, even if he didn't get to win anything in more than 100 played games: "I've given my soul to Inter, I really regret we haven't won a trophy though. I'm sorry because I've dreamed about this shirt since I'm a child and not being able to get the satisfaction to win something with Inter has stayed on my mind".

How does he explain it? "There was too much confusion. It's difficult when there are so many changes in the same time and even for Suning who is on the other side of the world, it would be better with an Italian owner like Moratti".


"So many changes on the bench? A consequence, according to me, from the confusion. This also brings bad results. The best example is Mancini: if even someone like him leaves in August, it means things aren't going well. He's a great coach like Bielsa. In 2015 we were at the first spot in the first part of the season before getting a bad moment: I think we weren't ready to be there on top. This jersey is and wasn't for everyone. Being at Inter also means there's some kind of pressure not easy to handle and the more years without winning go by, the bigger this pressure gets".

From his Inter to the current one, who's fighting for a Champions League spot: "Is it possible? Roma got back together, Lazio isn't out of the race and Milan is getting back. It won't be easy but Inter can't allow to fail again or it would be disastrous. Spalletti is a great coach and has been immediately clear, telling me that he needed players with good passing for his defense and midfield so I would have to evaluate my possibilities. He offered me to try to be his trequartista but I should have adapted and It would have been really difficult".

"I had a lot of opportunities last summer, in Spain and Mexico but this is a great club. Would I get back to Italy? No, no. There's only Inter for me in Serie A. How is the league? I thought it would be easier but the level is really high, there's a lot of concurrence in my team. We could win the league but there are four teams competing for it".

據《米蘭體育報》報導，國際米蘭現在距離簽下祖雲達斯球員K.艾沙莫亞僅剩一步之遙。

K.艾沙莫亞和祖記的合同將在本賽季結束後到期，這位加納球星可以勝任多個位置，國際米蘭對他很感興趣，藍黑軍團最近正在追求K.艾沙莫亞。

《米蘭體育報》稱，國際米蘭和K.艾沙莫亞已經達成了協議，雙方可能會在近期進行會面，敲定合同，並簽署協議。

在薪資待遇方面，《米蘭體育報》稱，國際米蘭向K.艾沙莫亞提供了一份為期3年的合同，稅後年薪為350萬歐元。

“We need a win against Verona to exercise mentally and get in the right condition ahead of the Milan derby. We need to think of Verona first, then Milan.

“I wanted to offer my congratulations to Stefano Vecchi and our Primavera side for the Viareggio Cup win. They were able to win a difficult tournament.

“I need to be able to get the most out of the players and with them giving their all, you can reach the most important position that there is. This team has shown that it has potential.

“San Siro is our home. When Inter are playing, the 60,000 expected tomorrow must help.”

Luciano Spalletti insists he will be Inter coach next season, despite the departure of technical co-ordinator Walter Sabatini.

Sabatini left his role with the Nerazzurri and owners Suning Group this week amid reports he had grown frustrated with the financial constraints imposed on the club.

His departure has also cast some doubt on the future of Spalletti, whose contract at San Siro expires at the end of next season.

However, the 59-year-old is adamant he will be at the club for 2018-19, during which he hopes to be involved in Champions League football.

"The renewal issue doesn't interest me. I know I'll be here next year. I don't see any problem," he told a news conference ahead of Saturday's Serie A clash with Hellas Verona.

"I like Milan as a place, I like Inter. I'm happy to keep working here. If we don't reach our objective this season, we'll always carry that with us.

"I'm very sorry for Sabatini because I know his human and professional qualities, but Inter are still at the forefront of everything and we have to continue working for the future with a common goal."

Sabatini's departure came after a mixed review of his recruitment policy, with shrewd loan deals for Rafinha and Joao Cancelo undermined by the struggles of big-money signings Geoffrey Kondogbia, Joao Mario and Gabriel Barbosa.

Spalletti, though, says he is monitoring the future of those three players with a view to bringing them back at the end of the season if they impress in their loan spells.

"Judgements are being made in the transfer market," he said. "We have some players in the squad that we don't own and there are others that we do own who aren't here. There's a lot to evaluate.

"One [we're looking at] is Kondogbia, who is doing very well. If they [Valencia] don't exercise the purchase option... it depends on them.

"[Yuto] Nagatomo and Joao Mario have played some very interesting matches. Gabigol has scored, when it was said he couldn't."

He added that Rafinha has made it clear he would like to extend his stay in Italy, where he is on loan from Barcelona.

"Rafinha wants to stay here," he said. "There are a lot of signs to confirm that. When I've asked him if he likes Inter, and Milan, he's said 'yes'."

Asamoah searching for a house in Milan as Inter move gets closer

The departure of Technical Director Walter Sabatini has not slowed down Inter’s transfer dealings.

With the imminent arrival of striker Lautaro Martinez and Dutch defender Stefan de Vrij, comes news that Ivorian full-back Kwadwo Asamoah is looking for a house in Milan ahead of a summer move to the Nerazzurri.

The 29-year-old is out of contract at Juventus at the end of the season and according to Gazzetta dello Sport, he has accepted the offer from Inter Sporting Director Piero Ausilio of a three-year contract worth €3M-a-season.

Juventus had hoped he would commit to a new deal in Turin but it seems the thought of joining a new club with a new project has made up his mind.

The pink journal also states this morning the coach Luciano Spalletti is also close to signing a contract extension at Appiano Gentile. The Tuscan tactician’s current deal expires in 2019 and Inter hope to get the new agreement finalised by the end of April.

It was the second straight win for Luciano Spalletti’s Inter who returned to action after the international break with a 3-0 triumph against Hellas Verona. “We made a big impact on the game, that was what we needed to do,” said the Coach. “We also managed the game well thanks to the start that we made and that meant we spent less energy. Everything was done well, apart from a few moments when we found space and didn’t capitalise when they were thrown off balance. We could have also been a bit less extravagant at times to give the fans more goals to celebrate, we must always be switched on at this stage of the season.”

“We’re a strong side but we need to show our quality with consistency. It’s on me to find a solution that allows everyone to be at their best. In reality, the team practically never really changed course, there was a winless run where we got too many decisions wrong. We needed to do something different in attack in terms how ruthless we were and with our finishing. Because of this, I didn’t want to change too much because there was a risk of going off track.”

Focus then turned to individuals. “Icardi is doing very well and he always wants to play to help to team but I preferred to take him off as a precaution because you can have more issues at this stage of the season. Rafinha is a player who knows how to bring the ball out with it at his feet, he knows when to play it and when to keep it. Brozovic is doing very well in a deeper role and he’s bringing the tidiness and quality that was missing in that position.”

Inevitably, the final comment was on the #DerbyMilano: “We want to win, both teams will want to win because it’s a match that can determine a lot.”

‘Il Divino’ Jonathan: “I Miss Italy & Inter”

Once he was dubbed “The Next Maicon” but today Jonathan plays in Atletico Paranaense and he is doing well as FcInterNews spoke to him in this exclusivel interview:

How is life going in Brazil?

“Here I am happy. I’m un a team that allows me to express myself better as I continue my career. The environment is amazing. All the guys, and the management, are friends. I was able to play well last season. After the injury with Inter, only a few would have bet on me. Instead, I practically went on the field more than all my team-mates, for hours, during the season.”

You have renewed with Atletico Paranaense and you are playing great. What are your short-term goals?

“I hope to continue doing well. The squad has strengthened. So many quality players have arrived. I want to finish my career here. I signed until 2020 for this.”

What memories do you have of your time at Inter?

“I miss Italy and I miss Inter. In addition to the people I met. The Nerazzurri were very important to me. I learned so many things about Italian football, especially at a tactical level. I’m happy with my career. Too bad for the injury that left other European teams doubting my recovery, and that I would not play well again. For this reason I have to thank Atletico Paranaense again, which allowed me to try everything to be competitive at a high level.”

Do you still follow the Nerazzurri?

“Yes, even if I do not quite can’t follow as much as I want because of the timing”.

What do you think of the current Inter?

“Inter started very well. I think they could have done even better. All the players would like to wear the Nerazzurri shirt. I think it’s just a matter of time until Inter to return to winning everything as they’ve done throughout their history.”

One of your interviews has remained in history. When you said: “Inter lose and it’s Jonathan’s fault. It’s raining and it’s Jonathan’s fault.” What started that outburst?

“Other guys were wrong and nobody criticized them harshly like they did me. At that time when we lost and I made some mistakes, so I ended up on the bench. So I came to say that phrase, and it was a mess.”

Did you like being called Il Divino?

“I’m sure this nickname was given to me by someone who wanted to make fun of me. Even today on social media they call me that, for fun. I do not care. It does not hurt me, neither does it any good. It doesn’t change anything.”

Do you have a message to the fans?

“I send them a warm hug and a big greeting. They are fond of me. I thank them and say that they will remain forever in my heart, along with Inter.”
